name: Nowruz
traditions: Setting the Haft-sin table with seven symbolic items; jumping over bonfires (Chaharshanbe Suri); spring cleaning; visiting elders; 13-day celebration.
historicalBackground: Nowruz has been celebrated for over 3,000 years, rooted in Zoroastrian traditions. It marks the Persian New Year and the spring equinox.
description: The Persian New Year celebrating the spring equinox, observed across Iran, Central Asia, and diaspora communities with ancient Zoroastrian traditions.
